name: Fiji
avoid: November–April is wetter, more humid, and overlaps the South Pacific cyclone season, so ferry schedules and sea conditions are less reliable.
Christmas, New Year, and Australian/NZ school-holiday weeks push up west-coast resort prices and can sell out popular Yasawa ferry sectors.
Sunday service is quieter in many towns and villages, so market browsing, buses, and cultural visits need a bit more pre-planning.
stopoverNote: For trips under 6 days, keep the plan on Viti Levu plus one west-coast island group; doing both Yasawas and Suva makes the pace feel transfer-heavy.

cities.name: Suva
cities.notes: Fiji's capital is the practical culture-and-rainforest finale for the itinerary, with the Fiji Museum, Colo-i-Suva, government-era architecture, and easy Fiji Link flights back to Nadi.

cities.name: Nadi
cities.notes: Nadi is the international arrival hub for temple visits, Sabeto attractions, Port Denarau departures, and a simple first or last night on Viti Levu.

cities.name: Mamanuca Islands
cities.notes: The Mamanucas are Fiji's easiest postcard-island stay from Port Denarau, with clear lagoons, resort reefs, and day cruises to sandbars and surf breaks.

cities.name: Yasawa Islands
cities.notes: The Yasawas deliver Fiji's slower island-hopping rhythm, with long beach stays, manta and shark snorkels, and the famous Sawa-i-Lau limestone caves.

cities.name: Coral Coast
cities.notes: The Coral Coast is the easiest land-based reef break between Nadi and Suva, mixing Sigatoka sights, village culture, and full-service beachfront resorts.

itinerary.sim: Vodafone Fiji prepaid is the safest all-round option for Viti Levu and major resort areas, while Digicel Fiji is the usual backup; download ferry vouchers and offline maps before sailing to the Yasawas.

Nadi.name: Nadi
Nadi.note: Use Nadi as the soft-landing base for Sabeto, temple stops, and Port Denarau logistics before you head onto the islands.

days.theme: Temple Colors, Sabeto Stops and a Gentle West-Coast Arrival

activities.name: Sri Siva Subramaniya Temple visit
activities.tip: Entry is typically donation-based or bundled with a short temple explanation; dress modestly and remove footwear.

activities.name: Garden of the Sleeping Giant
activities.tip: Adult tickets are commonly listed around FJ$35, and the orchid garden is easiest before midday heat builds.

activities.name: Sabeto Hot Springs and Mud Pool
activities.tip: Most adult walk-in tickets land around FJ$35–40; carry a change of clothes because transfers continue dusty and wet.

activities.name: Wailoaloa Beach sunset walk

hotels.name: Hilton Fiji Beach Resort and Spa

Mamanuca Islands.name: Mamanuca Islands
Mamanuca Islands.note: This is the easiest lagoon-and-resort block from Port Denarau, so it works best as one island stay with one full snorkel or sailing day.

days.theme: Port Denarau Departure, Resort Reef Time and a Lagoon Sunset

activities.name: South Sea Cruises or Malolo Cat transfer from Port Denarau to a Mamanuca resort
activities.tip: Adult ferry sectors to islands such as Plantation, Musket Cove, and Castaway commonly land around FJ$135–165 depending on the stop.

activities.name: House-reef snorkel at Plantation Island or Musket Cove
Kayak or stand-up paddle rental
Sunset cruise arranged by the resort

days.theme: Sailing Day, Snorkeling and Mamanuca Sandbars

activities.name: South Sea Cruises Seaspray sailing trip to Modriki and nearby sand cays
activities.tip: Adult day-trip pricing is usually around FJ$290–310 with lunch and snorkeling gear included.

activities.name: Introductory or single-tank dive with a Mamanuca resort dive centre
activities.tip: A resort dive usually prices near FJ$210–260 depending on whether gear hire is bundled.

activities.name: Cloud 9 floating platform boat outing
activities.tip: Boat-only transfers often start around FJ$160–200 before food or bar credit.

meals: Terrace Restaurant at Malolo Island Resort

Yasawa Islands.name: Yasawa Islands
Yasawa Islands.note: Yasawa time pays off only if you slow down a little, so keep one cave-or-manta day and one reef-or-village day rather than trying to island-hop too aggressively.

days.theme: Yasawa Flyer Transfer, Blue Water Check-In and an Easy First Snorkel

activities.name: Awesome Adventures Fiji Yasawa Flyer ferry into the Yasawas
activities.tip: Most adult sectors or pass-equivalent one-way segments price around FJ$159–199 depending on how far north you sail.

activities.name: Beach snorkel from Octopus Resort or Barefoot Manta Island Resort
Guided village visit with kava ceremony
activities.tip: Many Yasawa resorts charge roughly FJ$35–50 and ask guests to cover shoulders and knees.

days.theme: Sawa-i-Lau Caves, Mantas or Reef Sharks

activities.name: Sawa-i-Lau Caves guided visit
activities.tip: Adult cave visits are commonly priced near FJ$75 before boat transfer charges from your resort.

activities.name: Manta-ray snorkel with Mantaray Island Resort (seasonal)
activities.tip: Expect roughly FJ$89–109 for the guided water session in peak manta months, usually May to October.

activities.name: Reef shark snorkel with Barefoot Kuata
activities.tip: The signature shark-snorkel trip is typically listed around FJ$149 and usually includes guide support and safety briefing.

meals: The Chef's Table at Paradise Cove Resort

Coral Coast.name: Coral Coast
Coral Coast.note: Use Coral Coast as the land-based reset after the islands: one nature-and-culture day and one slower reef-or-resort day is the sweet spot.

days.theme: Sigatoka Heritage, Sand Dunes and a Reefside Evening

activities.name: Sigatoka Sand Dunes National Park
activities.tip: Adult entry is usually around FJ$10 and is easiest in the morning before the dunes heat up.

activities.name: Sigatoka Market and town stop
Coral Coast reef snorkel arranged by your resort
activities.tip: Expect roughly FJ$60–100 for a guided lagoon snorkel with gear on the Coral Coast.

days.theme: Village Waterfall, Wildlife Park and a Slower South-Coast Afternoon

activities.name: Biausevu Waterfall village visit
activities.tip: Most operators collect about FJ$25–35 per adult for the village guide and waterfall access, plus a small sevusevu contribution if requested.

activities.name: Kula WILD Adventure Park
activities.tip: Adult tickets are commonly listed around FJ$50.

activities.name: Natadola Beach swim stop

Suva.name: Suva
Suva.note: Suva is best used as a culture-heavy last stop, especially if you want museums and rainforest before an easy Fiji Link hop back to Nadi.

days.theme: Museum Collections, Market Life and a Rainforest Detour

activities.name: Fiji Museum
activities.tip: Adult admission is commonly around FJ$15 and pairs well with a short Thurston Gardens walk.

activities.name: Suva Municipal Market and Victoria Parade walk
Colo-i-Suva Forest Park swimming holes
activities.tip: Budget around FJ$5–10 for entry or local guide costs depending on the trail area you use.

activities.name: Fiji Link flight from Suva back to Nadi for onward departures
activities.tip: Nausori-to-Nadi one-way fares often start around FJ$99 and can climb toward FJ$180 on busier dates.

connections.method: Local bus or short taxi ride to Wailoaloa, Namaka, or Denarau-side hotels.
South Sea Cruises or Malolo Cat ferry from Port Denarau to resort islands such as Plantation, Musket Cove, Castaway, or Malolo.
Awesome Adventures Fiji Yasawa Flyer northbound service, or a same-day reposition via Port Denarau if your resort is not on the through-ferry route.
Yasawa Flyer back to Port Denarau followed by a Sunbeam or Pacific Transport coach to Sigatoka or Korotogo.
Pacific Transport or Sunbeam road coach along Queens Road; private transfers are faster but much pricier.
Fiji Link domestic flight from Nausori (Suva) to Nadi, then a short terminal transfer for onward international departures.

extras: Most Fiji resort pricing and ferry check-ins work smoothly with credit cards, but keep small FJD cash for village visits, bus fares, market snacks, and sevusevu contributions.
Reef-safe sunscreen, a dry bag, and a basic rash guard are worth carrying because many day boats leave gear exposed and midday UV is intense even in the dry season.
Awesome Adventures Fiji and South Sea Cruises both enforce checked-baggage limits on some island sectors, so pack soft luggage if you plan to ferry-hop rather than stay on one island.
Sunday mornings are quieter around towns and villages, and some attractions or shops trim hours, so make ferry, market, and cultural plans a little earlier in the trip.

Nadi: Garden of the Sleeping Giant Fiji
Coral Coast: Outrigger Fiji Beach Resort Coral Coast
